data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Drops as Safety Fears Persist, Even With Another Probe Closed
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) edged lower during Thursday's pre-market session despite the U.S. auto safety watchdog closing another of its probes into the company's vehicles. This comes as concerns about the safety of the automaker's vehicles remain.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

TSLQ: built for a short position on TSLA

NHTSA Closes 2022 Tesla Investigation

On Thursday, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) announced that it had shut down its preliminary probe from 2022 into 695,000 Tesla vehicles. The regulator said the sudden deceleration incidents that sparked the investigation had dwindled to a trickle.

The investigation had covered Tesla's Model 3 and Model Y vehicles. However, NHTSA found that the defect did not cause the vehicles to drift out of their lanes. The vehicles also did not lose enough space behind the next car to cause an accident.

Moreover, in early 2022, Tesla released software updates to fix the bug causing the incident, NHTSA said. The new closure comes just days after the safety watchdog similarly closed its July 2023 probe into certain Model 3 and Model Y vehicles. The investigation had focused on power steering loss in the vehicles.

Tesla Continues to Face Scrutiny over Vehicle Safety

Meanwhile, despite the recent closures of investigations into Tesla vehicles, the EV giant has continued to face questions about the safety of its technology.

Last month, two U.S. senators called on the NHTSA to review crash data involving Tesla's driver-assist software. The call came after Reuters reported that the automaker widely inflates the safety numbers of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology.

Moreover, the NHTSA has been investigating Tesla's FSD systems since 2024. It launched a probe into 2.88 million units in October 2025 and escalated it in March over concerns about the FSD system's performance in poor weather conditions.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy or Sell Today?

On Wall Street, Tesla's shares now carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 11 Buys, 14 Holds, and three Sells issued by 28 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average TSLA price target of $403.66 suggests about 5% downside risk from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: AI Is the Ultimate Growth Tailwind for Eli Lilly (LLY). Here's Why
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI) could prove to be a huge long-term growth tailwind for Eli Lilly (LLY), as it's transforming the company's multi-decade trajectory. AI is changing how the company discovers, develops, and brings new drugs to market. Rather than simply accelerating existing research, AI is helping transform Lilly from a traditional drugmaker into a more scalable, AI-powered engineering platform.

data.title: Bending Spoons Stock (BSP) Pops 40% after IPO — Here's Why Investors Are Buying
data.description:
Shares of Italian technology company Bending Spoons (BSP) surged nearly 40% on their first day of trading. The company priced its IPO above the expected range, reflecting strong investor demand. The stock gained another 2% in premarket trading on Thursday. Investors are optimistic about the company's simple strategy of acquiring software platforms and using AI to enhance its products and drive future growth. The strong debut is especially notable as many software companies have struggled to attract investor interest in recent years, making Bending Spoons one of the strongest SaaS IPOs in recent times.

data.title: 'Utterly False,' Musk Denies SpaceX AI Phone Report, Stock Tumbles
data.description:
Elon Musk denied a Wall Street Journal report claiming that SpaceX (SPCX) was developing an AI-powered handset-like device. Musk's "utterly false" response on X sent SPCX stock down 7.8% on Wednesday. The space exploration company's shares have given up most of their post-IPO gains and are now down 2.1% since listing.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

According to people familiar with the matter, the prototype runs on a custom operating system and integrates xAI technology. The WSJ report also said that the AI device uses Qualcomm's (QCOM) Snapdragon chipset, which sent the stock briefly higher before Musk denied claims. The AI-powered handset is also sleeker than Apple's (AAPL) iPhone.

The device was reportedly shown to some investors ahead of the company's planned initial public offering (IPO) in June. However, the report cautioned that the project was in an early stage and could be dropped completely.

Can SpaceX Expand into Smartphones?

SpaceX has invested billions of dollars to expand beyond its rocket launches and Starlink satellite internet business. It is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and integrating xAI's Grok AI model into its broader ecosystem. The company also envisions space-based data centers as Musk aims to make SpaceX a major player in the AI industry.

Although Musk denied the Wall Street Journal report, speculation about a SpaceX phone has surfaced before. Reuters reported in February that SpaceX was considering developing a mobile device that would connect directly to its Starlink satellite internet network and could compete with smartphones. However, Musk also dismissed those reports.

Meanwhile, in January, Musk had said that a Starlink phone was "not out of the question" in the future. He added that if SpaceX builds one, it would be very different from today's smartphones.

Moreover, Reuters recently reported that SpaceX is exploring plans to offer a mobile network and is in talks with Charter Communications (CHTR) to use its ground infrastructure for phone traffic. The company already works with T-Mobile (TMUS) to provide direct-to-cell satellite connectivity through its Starlink network.

Is It Too Late to Buy SpaceX?

Analysts remain divided on SpaceX's long-term outlook after its massive IPO rally. On TipRanks, SPCX has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on four Buys, three Holds, and one Sell rating. The average SpaceX price target of $216.83 implies 37.6% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Adobe Stock (ADBE) Jumps after Top Analyst Upgrades It to Buy and Raises Price Target
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) rose over 2% in pre-market trading on Thursday after HSBC turned bullish on the stock. Five-star analyst Stephen Bersey upgraded Adobe to Buy from Hold and raised his price target to $308 from $282, implying about 46% upside from current levels. Bersey said the company's latest earnings results show little evidence that AI rivals are hurting its business, despite growing competition from AI-powered design tools.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Bersey is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #519 out of the 12,346 analysts tracked. He has a 73% success rate and an average return per rating of 19.80%.

Why HSBC Turned More Bullish on Adobe

Bersey said Adobe continues to deliver steady growth despite the rapid rise of AI-powered design tools. The analyst noted that Adobe's fiscal second-quarter revenue climbed 13% year over year to $6.62 billion, while the company also raised its full-year outlook after another strong quarter.

The analyst added that Adobe's backlog continues to grow. Remaining performance obligations increased 13% from a year ago, showing that customers are still committing to the company's products.

HSBC believes those numbers highlight Adobe's biggest strength. Bersey said the company's software has remained "sticky" despite growing AI competition. Customers continue to use Adobe's products every day because they are already part of their workflow.

Adobe is also starting to generate more revenue from AI. Bersey noted that the company's AI-first revenue has tripled over the past year, although it still makes up only about 2% of quarterly revenue. He believes this shows customers are using AI to improve Adobe's products rather than replace them.

Based on that view, Bersey expects Adobe to keep growing as it expands its AI tools while holding on to its large customer base.

Is Adobe Stock a Buy?

According to TipRanks, Adobe stock has a Hold consensus rating based on seven Buy, 16 Hold, and two Sell ratings. The average ADBE analyst price target of $257.93 implies about 22% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Futures Tick Higher as Tech Selloff Deepens, Jobs Data in Focus
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher ahead of Thursday's session. However, weakness in technology stocks continued to weigh on investor sentiment after markets started the second half of the year on a weak note. Investors are also awaiting the June jobs report, with economists expecting the U.S. economy to have added 115,000 jobs last month.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.17%, 0.12%, and 0.06%, respectively, at 1:20 a.m. EDT on July 2.

Oil prices remained under pressure amid easing Middle East tensions. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 0.35% at $70.66 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell 0.27% to $68.28.

During Wednesday's regular session, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.66% and the S&P 500 slipped 0.22%, weighed down by sharp declines in chip stocks. Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) plunged more than 10%, while Intel (INTC) fell over 9%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones briefly hit a new intraday record before ending little changed.

In key economic reports, investors will monitor the June non-farm payrolls report, the unemployment rate, initial jobless claims, and May factory orders.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Turn Mixed as Traders Await Jobs Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Wednesday evening after Wall Street entered the new month on a weak note. Also, traders awaited the release of fresh U.S. jobs data. Futures on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were down 0.11% and 0.06%, respectively, at 6:45 p.m. EDT on July 1, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) futures were up 0.05%.

data.title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp Criticizes 'Tokenmaxxing' as AI Costs Rise
data.description:
Alex Karp, the CEO of AI software company Palantir (PLTR), is pushing back against the token-based AI pricing model used by frontier labs. In an interview with CNBC, Karp stated that something has "gone completely wrong" with the way companies are being encouraged to spend money on tokens. His comments come as businesses are becoming more sensitive to rising AI costs, especially as newer models become more expensive and token usage continues to grow.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The issue is that many AI providers charge customers based on token usage, which generally means the amount of text or data processed by an AI model. However, Palantir argues that this can push companies toward more usage rather than better business outcomes. In a post on X, Palantir criticized what it called "tokenmaxxing" by arguing that enterprises should care less about how many tokens they consume and more about whether AI actually improves their operations.

As a result, more companies are looking at alternatives to closed AI systems. For example, some are using open-weight models, which are cheaper and give customers more flexibility. Meanwhile, others are building custom AI models designed for their own data and workflows. This is where Palantir is trying to position itself.

Nvidia and Palantir Announce a Collaboration

Earlier this week, Palantir announced a collaboration with chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) that uses Nvidia's open-source Nemotron models to help government customers build custom AI systems. According to Karp, technical customers want control over their AI infrastructure. He also said that companies and governments should not hand over too much control of their future AI systems to outside model providers.

Is PLTR Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on PLTR stock based on 13 Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $181 per share implies 44% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
